claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to know a bit about your child (or children). 

**First, how old is your child?**

The age requirements are:
- **Under 16**: Generally qualifies automatically (subject to other conditions)
- **16-19**: May qualify if they're in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Not eligible for Child Benefit

Please tell me:
1. How old is your child?
2. What is your child's name? (This helps me keep track if you have multiple children)

If you have more than one child, we can check eligibility for each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex, who's 7 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me work through Alex's eligibility with you.

**Alex is 7 years old**, which means he's under 16. That's a good start! Now let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since Alex is 7 years old, he's obviously not working full-time (this check is mainly for older children). Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right** (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

At 7 years old, this is unlikely, but I need to check. Can you confirm that Alex is NOT receiving these types of benefits?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's only 7, so he doesn't get anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Again, since Alex is only 7 years old, he's definitely not in an apprenticeship (this is another check for older teenagers). Let me move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now we need to check your residency status. 

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

This is about YOUR residency, not Alex's. You can meet this requirement in several ways:
- You normally live in the UK, OR
- You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
- Various other special circumstances (like having pre-settled status and working, or being a Crown servant abroad)

Can you tell me about your residency situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, let me check if you might still qualify under special circumstances.

**Are any of the following true for you?**

1. **Crown servant abroad**: Are you working abroad as a Crown servant (such as a diplomat, member of HM Forces, or similar government role)?

2. **Living in certain qualifying countries**: Are you living in one of the qualifying countries where you can still claim Child Benefit? (These are typically limited to specific situations)

3. **EU Settlement Scheme status**: Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme? (This would apply if you're an EEA or Swiss national or family member)

Could you tell me more about your situation abroad? What country are you in, and what's the nature of your work or stay there?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad - I'm not a Crown servant or diplomat, and I don't have any special EU settlement status. It's just a regular move abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, I need to let you know the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## **Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Result**

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex.

### **Why you're not eligible:**

While Alex meets all the child-related requirements (he's 7 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**.

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria if pre-settled), OR
- Be a Crown servant working abroad, OR
- Live in certain qualifying countries

Since you're living abroad in a regular situation without any of these special circumstances, you unfortunately don't qualify.

### **What you might consider:**

- If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK, you could apply for Child Benefit at that time
- If you believe your situation might involve special circumstances not covered in our discussion, you could contact HMRC directly for advice

### **Need more information?**

You can find more details about residency requirements at: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

I'm sorry this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. If you have any questions about this decision or if your circumstances change in the future, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old, which falls under the qualifying age (under 16). He is not working full-time, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any special circumstances (not a Crown servant, not living in qualifying countries, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme). Child Benefit requires that the claimant normally lives in the UK or meets one of the special circumstance exceptions. Since the claimant fails to meet the residency requirement, they are ineligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 7), who would otherwise qualify for Child Benefit based on age and other child-related criteria. However, the claimant is ineligible because they do not meet the UK residency requirements, as they are living abroad without any qualifying special circumstances.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

Child Benefit requires that the claimant normally lives in the UK or meets one of the special circumstance exceptions. Since the claimant fails to meet the residency requirement, they are ineligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
